preview_text:
The model favors France at 69%, assigning Paraguay only a 9% chance of victory. The underdog's path to three points is narrow but nonzero; France enters as a clear favorite on the neutral ground.

The 22% draw probability suggests a match unlikely to be decided by a wide margin. France's win probability outweighs the draw by roughly three-to-one, indicating the model expects France to be the stronger side but acknowledges competitive resistance. A Paraguay upset or stalemate cannot be ruled out on the day.

The market consensus skews more heavily toward France, pricing a win at 81% compared to the model's 69%—a gap of roughly 12 percentage points. The sportsbook consensus also compresses the draw to 14%, well below the model's 22%. Polymarket prices align closely with the book consensus. The dashboard does not adjudicate which estimate is more accurate; the divergence reflects genuine uncertainty about match conditions and relative team strength.
